task_completion.description: Survey titled 'SaaS Pricing Strategy 2026' and question group 'Pricing & Features' are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LimeSurvey interface shows the survey title 'SaaS Pricing Strategy 2026' and a question group named 'Pricing & Features' in the left sidebar structure panel or main dashboard.

task_completion.description: Question 1 is created with Code 'PREF' and Type 'List (Radio)'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A question with the code 'PREF' is visible, and its question type is clearly set to 'List (Radio)' in the question settings or summary.

task_completion.description: Question 1 text contains an HTML table comparing the three pricing tiers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The question text editor (in WYSIWYG or source mode) or the survey preview for PREF shows a structured table containing 'Starter' ($29/mo), 'Pro' ($79/mo), and 'Enterprise' (Custom).

task_completion.description: Question 1 has the correct answer options.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The answer options editor for the PREF question lists 'Starter Tier', 'Pro Tier', and 'Enterprise Tier'.

task_completion.description: Question 2 is created with Code 'INTEG' and Type 'Multiple choice'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A question with the code 'INTEG' is visible, and its question type is set to 'Multiple choice'.

task_completion.description: Question 2 has the correct answer options (subquestions).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The subquestions editor for the INTEG question lists 'Salesforce', 'Slack', 'HubSpot', and 'No integrations needed'.

task_completion.description: The 'No integrations needed' option is configured as an exclusive option.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the question settings for INTEG (typically under the 'Logic' tab), the 'Exclusive option' field is filled with the subquestion code that corresponds to the 'No integrations needed' option.

integrity.description: The agent used the LimeSurvey web interface to create the survey.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LimeSurvey admin interface being navigated and forms being filled out in a web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts or direct database queries to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing SQL commands, API calls, or scripts interacting with the LimeSurvey backend.

integrity.description: The HTML table was genuinely created within the LimeSurvey editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The table is seen being constructed or pasted into the LimeSurvey rich text editor or source code view, rather than being mocked up in an external text editor without transferring it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Survey Title: 'SaaS Pricing Strategy 2026'. Q1 Code: 'PREF', Type: List (Radio), must contain an HTML table with strings '<table', '$29', '$79', 'Starter', 'Pro'. Q2 Code: 'INTEG', Type: Multiple choice. Exclusive option text: 'No integrations needed'.
